label: Gaussian processes

node.label: Gaussian processes
node.definition: A Gaussian process places a probability distribution over functions and uses a kernel to couple observations, interpolate predictions, and quantify posterior uncertainty.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Gaussian processes is to connect Kernels & implicit feature maps, Bayesian & approximate inference to downstream capabilities including Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection, Scientific ML, simulators & digital twins, Weather, climate & geospatial forecasting.
examples: substrate for Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection
substrate for Scientific ML, simulators & digital twins
substrate for Weather, climate & geospatial forecasting
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Kernels & implicit feature maps, Bayesian & approximate inference.

dependencies.rationale: Gaussian processes is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Kernels & implicit feature maps.
Gaussian processes is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Bayesian & approximate inference.
Gaussian processes consumes, emits, or organizes information using Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs.
